title: Minimize the admission of containers with capabilities assigned

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers with capabilities assigned
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers with capabilities.
input_row.rationale: Containers run with a default set of capabilities as assigned by the Container Runtime. Capabilities are parts of the rights generally granted on a Linux system to the root user. In many cases applications running in containers do not require any capabilities to operate, so from the perspective of the principal of least privilege use of capabilities should be minimized.
input_row.audit: Use the following command to list SCCs that drop all capabilities from containers: oc get scc -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.requiredDropCapabilities[]?|any(. == "ALL"; .)) | .metadata.name' Verify at least one SCC is returned.
input_row.remediation: Review the use of capabilities in applications running on your cluster. Where a namespace contains applications which do not require any Linux capabilities to operate, consider adding a SCC which forbids the admission of containers which do not drop all capabilities.
input_row.impact: Pods with containers which require capabilities to operate will not be permitted.
input_row.default_value: By default, OpenShift includes three SCCs that drop all container capabilities: "hostnetwork-v2" "nonroot-v2" "restricted-v2"

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access Security Context Constraints (SCC) in OpenShift:**
   - Use the OpenShift CLI `oc` to connect to your OpenShift cluster.

2. **Run the Command:**
   - Use the command: `oc get scc -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.requiredDropCapabilities[]?|any(. == "ALL"; .)) | .metadata.name'`
   - This retrieves the list of Security Context Constraints that are configured to drop all container capabilities.

3. **What to Verify/Validate:**
   - Ensure that at least one SCC is returned by the command. 
   - Check for any SCCs that effectively drop all capabilities (e.g., the output should list SCCs such as "hostnetwork-v2", "nonroot-v2", "restricted-v2").

4. **Expected Secure Configuration:**
   - You should see at least one SCC in the output named in the default settings (e.g., "restricted-v2").

5. **How to Confirm Compliance:**
   - If the command returns at least one SCC that drops all capabilities, the control is compliant. If no SCCs are listed, non-compliance exists, and further review and remediation would be necessary to create or configure one that limits capabilities appropriately.
